slides.title: Community and Communication
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 4 English, Unit 4: Community and Communication. Welcome to Unit 4! This unit is all about community and communication — the many ways people share news, ideas, and help with one another. You will read about a library service, a young reporter, and how a whole town shares information. You will learn strong new words like service, deliver, knowledge, information, discover, and priority, and you will use them to talk and write about your own community. By the end, you will be ready to share a message clearly, just like Amal does in this unit's story, “The Circular Plan”. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Talk about services and communication in your community, using new Unit 4 words
Describe places and things using There is and There are
State facts about your town using the present simple (“Mail carriers deliver letters daily”)
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to talk about services and communication in your community, using new unit 4 words. you will also describe places and things using there is and there are. you will also state facts about your town using the present simple (“mail carriers deliver letters daily”). Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: service, deliver, information, thought, discover, knowledge. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
bullets: The Library That Came to Us
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes The Library That Came to Us and Selma the Young Reporter. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Countable and Uncountable Nouns
bullets: Some nouns we can count one by one — one book, two books, three books. We call these countable nouns. Other nouns we cannot count as separate items — like...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Countable and Uncountable Nouns. Some nouns we can count one by one — one book, two books, three books. We call these countable nouns. Other nouns we cannot count as separate items — like water, rice, or information. We call these uncountable nouns. Knowing the difference helps you choose the right words, so your sentences make sense. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Speaking 1: Describe a Service in Your Community
Writing 1: Instructions: “How to Share News in My Community”
Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Describe a Service in Your Community. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
